title: Trump and Iran Face Off in Iran War Negotiations
summary: As the United States and Iran make a second attempt at a deal, their negotiating styles are on a collision course.

title: The Touska, the Iranian-Flagged Ship Seized by U.S. Forces, Was Under Sanctions
summary: Marines are searching thousands of containers aboard the Touska, an Iranian cargo ship that the Navy disabled and seized on Sunday.

title: Israeli Soldier in Lebanon Sledgehammered a Statue of Jesus
summary: The military is investigating the soldier. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu expressed regret for any hurt caused to “believers in Lebanon and around the world.”

title: Tim Cook Will Step Down as Apple C.E.O.
summary: The longtime leader of the iPhone maker will be replaced by John Ternus, the company’s head of hardware engineering.

title: Who Is John Ternus, Apple’s Low-Profile Leader?
summary: John Ternus, a low-profile but influential executive at Apple, could be next in line to replace the company’s longtime chief executive, Tim Cook, if he steps aside.

title: Lori Chavez-DeRemer, Trump’s Labor Secretary, Steps Down Amid Internal Investigation
summary: Lori Chavez-DeRemer was facing multiple scandals and an internal inquiry into charges of misconduct.

title: Kash Patel Sues The Atlantic for $250 Million Over Article Claiming Excessive Drinking
summary: Mr. Patel accused the publication of defamation, asking for $250 million in damages. A spokeswoman for The Atlantic called the suit “meritless.”

title: Democrat Betty Yee Leaves the California Governor’s Race
summary: Xavier Becerra, who once struggled to gain traction, has found growing support after Eric Swalwell left the race amid sexual harassment allegations.

title: Why a Democratic Senate, Once Unthinkable, Is a Real Possibility
summary: Helped by a favorable national environment and strong candidate recruitment, Democrats are tied or ahead in four Republican-held seats, polls show.

title: Tracking the Virginia Redistricting Referendum Early Vote
summary: Voters in Virginia will decide on Tuesday whether to redraw the state’s congressional map in an attempt to eliminate all but one safe Republican district.

title: How Bruce the Parrot Landed Atop the Pecking Order, Without a Beak
summary: The kea gained fame for learning to use a pebble to groom himself. Scientists were astounded by his next innovation.

title: Canadian Tourist Is Killed in Shooting at Mexico’s Pyramids
summary: A gunman opened fire at the Teotihuacán archaeological site outside Mexico City before killing himself, the authorities said. Six Americans were among those injured, including two from gunshots.

title: Louisiana Killer’s Threats to Himself and Others Hinted at Future Violence
summary: Shamar Elkins had been suffering severe mental health problems, his family said. His deadly rampage, which killed eight children, has profoundly scarred a community facing rising domestic violence.

title: Singer D4vd Charged With Murder of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
summary: The singer is accused of sexually abusing Celeste Rivas Hernandez before killing the teenager with a sharp weapon and mutilating her body. He has pleaded not guilty.

title: The Onion Signs New Deal to Take Over Infowars
summary: A new deal, which would allow The Onion to use the Infowars name and website address, must be approved by a Texas judge.

title: London Braces for Disruption From Tube Drivers’ Strike
summary: The dispute over working conditions and hours is expected to cause widespread delays, stopping service on two lines.

title: Japan to Sell More Weapons Abroad, Breaking With Postwar Pacifism
summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi reversed limits on arms exports as Japan faces rising threats from China and unpredictability from its main ally, the United States.

title: ‘Earthset’ Is Captured on Video for First Time
summary: The Earth slips behind the moon in an iPhone video taken by Reid Wiseman during NASA’s Artemis II mission, a sequel to the iconic Earthrise photo taken by Apollo 8.

title: Blue Origin’s Failure May Hamstring NASA’s Moon Plans
summary: The space agency is counting on Jeff Bezos’ company to deliver equipment essential to the next moon landing, only two years away.

title: A Day in the Life of New York City’s Young Chess Stars
summary: Children from New York schools are consistently winning national chess championships, and programs dedicated to teaching young people the game are booming.

title: The Trump Administration Is Coming After Birth Control Access in a Terrifying New Way
summary: The new Title X guidance from the Trump administration mentions contraception only once.

title: Can Kevin Warsh Pass the Jerome Powell Test?
summary: The question of the central bank’s independence looms over the economy.

title: Jimmy Kimmel Refers to Kash Patel’s Job in the Past Tense
summary: The late night host called him “our soon-to-be former F.B.I. director” after publication of an article in The Atlantic alleging excessive drinking and unexplained absences.

title: Second Jury Finds Uber Responsible for Sexual Assault by a Driver
summary: The ride-hailing giant has now lost the first two of more than 3,000 pending federal lawsuits.

title: Gilgo Beach Killer Told His Wife That He Murdered 7 Women in Their Home
summary: Rex Heuermann, who pleaded guilty to murdering eight women, told his wife that he killed seven of the victims in a downstairs room in their Long Island home, she said in a Peacock documentary.

title: George Ariyoshi, 100, America’s First Governor of Asian Descent, Dies
summary: A Democrat, he served three terms as Hawaii’s governor (1974-86), rising out of a tough district of Honolulu. He tried to diversify an economy heavily dependent on tourism.

title: ‘Oh, My Baby’: Mother Mourns Son, 15, Whose Killing Was Filmed on Video
summary: Jaden Pierre was fatally shot at a Queens park in broad daylight, and bystanders captured the shooting on video. Hundreds attended a vigil for him on Monday night.
